title: Normalization of Samples for Genomic and Proteomic Applications
author: Opentrons

steps.title: Concentration normalization is a key component...
steps.instruction: Concentration normalization is a key component of many genomic and proteomic applications, such as NGS library prep. With this protocol, you can easily normalize the concentrations of samples in a 96-well plate from either 1.5ml snapcap tubes, or another 96-well plate. The transfers should be specified as an input .csv file formatted as shown here: ``` Sample number,Slot no number,Source well,Destination well,Start Concentration (ug/uL) Source,Final Concentraion (ug/ml) Destination,Sample volume (uL),Diluent Volume(ul),Total Volume(ul) Destination 1,4,A1,A1,5,2,40,60,100.0 2,4,C1,B1,3,2,67,33,100.0 3,4,D1,C1,10,2,20,80,100.0 4,4,E3,D1,20,2,10,90,100.0 ``` You can also download and edit [this template](https://opentrons-protocol-library-website.s3.amazonaws.com/custom-README-images/4883fc/example.xlsx). Be sure to export the `.xlsx` format to `.csv` format before inputting as the `input .csv file` below! ---

substeps.instruction: Concentration normalization is a key component of many genomic and proteomic applications, such as NGS library prep. With this protocol, you can easily normalize the concentrations of samples in a 96-well plate from either 1.5ml snapcap tubes, or another 96-well plate. The transfers should be specified as an input .csv file formatted as shown here: ``` Sample number,Slot no number,Source well,Destination well,Start Concentration (ug/uL) Source,Final Concentraion (ug/ml) Destination,Sample volume (uL),Diluent Volume(ul),Total Volume(ul) Destination 1,4,A1,A1,5,2,40,60,

``` You can also download and edit [this template](https://opentrons-protocol-library-website.s3.amazonaws.com/custom-README-images/4883fc/example.xlsx). Be sure to export the `.xlsx` format to `.csv` format before inputting as the `input .csv file` below! ---

steps.title: Diluent is transferred in the proper...
steps.instruction: Diluent is transferred in the proper amounts to each destination well of the end PCR plate using 1 tip for the entire set of transfers.

steps.title: The first sample is transferred to...
steps.instruction: The first sample is transferred to its specified destination well with pre-added diluent, the well is mixed 3x. After dispense, a blowout and touch tip are performed. The tip is then dropped.

steps.title: Step 2 is repeated for as...
steps.instruction: Step 2 is repeated for as many samples as specified in the .csv file.
